每日攝取益生菌/發酵食物.name: Take probiotics / fermented foods daily
每日攝取益生菌/發酵食物.description: Probiotics keep the gut microbiome balanced; fermented foods provide live cultures and short-chain fatty acids that support immunity and digestion.

觀察並記錄每日排便狀況.name: Track your daily bowel movements
觀察並記錄每日排便狀況.description: Stool form (the Bristol Stool Scale) is a real-time indicator of gut health; keeping a daily log helps you spot abnormalities early.

避免攝取過敏原食物.name: Avoid your food allergens
避免攝取過敏原食物.description: Personal allergens (such as gluten or dairy) can trigger chronic inflammation; identifying and avoiding them improves both gut health and mental well-being.

定期進行健康檢查.name: Get regular health checkups
定期進行健康檢查.description: Prevention beats treatment: annual checkups can catch chronic-disease risk markers before symptoms ever appear.

記錄家族病史風險.name: Record your family health history
記錄家族病史風險.description: Family medical history reveals genetic predispositions, guiding your prevention strategy and how often you should get screened.

每天攝取足夠膳食纖維.name: Eat enough dietary fiber every day
每天攝取足夠膳食纖維.description: Dietary fiber feeds beneficial gut bacteria, supporting regular bowel movements, stable blood sugar, and lower cholesterol.

餐前喝一杯溫水.name: Drink a glass of warm water before meals
餐前喝一杯溫水.description: Hydrating before meals stimulates gastric secretion and slows down eating, helping you control portions and improve digestion.

避免不必要的抗生素使用.name: Avoid unnecessary antibiotics
避免不必要的抗生素使用.description: Overusing antibiotics disrupts the gut microbiome; take them only on a doctor's advice to protect your microbial diversity.

練習腹式呼吸按摩腸道.name: Practice belly breathing to massage the gut
練習腹式呼吸按摩腸道.description: Diaphragmatic breathing mobilizes the diaphragm and internal organs and stimulates the parasympathetic nervous system, aiding gut motility and relieving stress.

攝取富含益生元的食物 (洋蔥/蒜).name: Eat prebiotic-rich foods (onion/garlic)
攝取富含益生元的食物 (洋蔥/蒜).description: Prebiotics are the food source for probiotics; onions, garlic, and other oligosaccharide-rich foods nourish beneficial gut bacteria.

每天早晨開窗通風.name: Open the windows for fresh air each morning
每天早晨開窗通風.description: Carbon dioxide and volatile compounds build up overnight and dull alertness; airing out the room each morning improves air quality and mental clarity.

整理工作桌面保持清爽.name: Keep your work desk tidy
整理工作桌面保持清爽.description: A tidy environment reduces visual distraction and cognitive load, supporting focus and better decision-making.

接觸大自然/戶外散步.name: Get into nature / take an outdoor walk
接觸大自然/戶外散步.description: Time in green spaces lowers cortisol and lifts mood — a low-cost health intervention known as the “green prescription.”

減少暴露於噪音環境.name: Reduce exposure to noisy environments
減少暴露於噪音環境.description: Chronic noise exposure raises blood pressure and damages hearing; actively reducing noise protects the cardiovascular and auditory systems.

每週進行數位環境大掃除.name: Do a weekly digital declutter
每週進行數位環境大掃除.description: Cleaning up files, unsubscribing from emails, and organizing your desktop icons reduces digital fatigue and information anxiety.

臥室保持完全黑暗 (助眠).name: Keep the bedroom completely dark (for sleep)
臥室保持完全黑暗 (助眠).description: Light suppresses melatonin secretion; a fully dark room helps you fall asleep faster and sleep more deeply.

種植室內植物淨化空氣.name: Grow indoor plants to purify the air
種植室內植物淨化空氣.description: Houseplants absorb some volatile organic compounds and raise humidity, while also providing a calming psychological effect.

調整符合人體工學的桌椅.name: Set up an ergonomic desk and chair
調整符合人體工學的桌椅.description: Properly adjusted desk and chair height reduces cumulative strain on the neck, shoulders, and lower back — a basic investment for anyone working long hours.

定期清理冰箱與儲藏室.name: Clean out the fridge and pantry regularly
定期清理冰箱與儲藏室.description: Removing expired food lowers foodborne-illness risk, and a well-organized pantry makes healthy eating choices clearer.

減少使用一次性塑膠.name: Cut down on single-use plastics
減少使用一次性塑膠.description: Using less plastic reduces microplastic exposure and environmental burden; bringing your own containers is the easiest way to start.

執行 168 間歇性斷食.name: Do 16:8 intermittent fasting
執行 168 間歇性斷食.description: A 16-hour fasting window lowers insulin and activates cellular autophagy — a common and easy-to-follow metabolic intervention.

每餐攝取一個拳頭的蔬菜.name: Eat a fist-sized serving of vegetables each meal
每餐攝取一個拳頭的蔬菜.description: Vegetables provide fiber, vitamins, and phytochemicals; eating enough of them is the cornerstone of a balanced diet.

減少精緻糖與澱粉攝取.name: Cut back on refined sugar and starch
減少精緻糖與澱粉攝取.description: Refined sugar and starch cause sharp blood-sugar swings; long-term excess raises the risk of metabolic syndrome.

吃飯細嚼慢嚥 (每口20下).name: Chew slowly (20 chews per bite)
吃飯細嚼慢嚥 (每口20下).description: Thorough chewing gives satiety signals time to register, helping you control portions and improving digestion and nutrient absorption.

維持血糖穩定 (先吃菜肉再吃飯).name: Keep blood sugar steady (veggies and protein before rice)
維持血糖穩定 (先吃菜肉再吃飯).description: Eating order affects the post-meal glucose response; having vegetables and protein first blunts the blood-sugar spike from starches.

每天喝足 2500cc 水.name: Drink 2500 cc of water every day
每天喝足 2500cc 水.description: Hydration affects metabolism, skin, and cognition; sipping small amounts throughout the day works better than drinking a lot at once.

每週實行一日無肉日.name: Have one meat-free day each week
每週實行一日無肉日.description: Skipping animal protein for one day lowers saturated-fat intake and environmental impact, while adding more plant diversity to your diet.

避免晚餐後進食 (消夜).name: No eating after dinner (late-night snacks)
避免晚餐後進食 (消夜).description: Eating close to bedtime disrupts digestion and sleep quality; an evening fast lets your metabolism rest and promotes fat burning.

攝取優質 Omega-3 油脂.name: Get quality omega-3 fats
攝取優質 Omega-3 油脂.description: Omega-3s help counter inflammation in the cardiovascular system and brain; common sources include oily fish, flaxseed, and walnuts.

閱讀食品營養標示.name: Read nutrition labels
閱讀食品營養標示.description: Understanding calories, sugar, sodium, and ingredient order is the first step to escaping the processed-food trap.

每餐都要有一份蛋白質.name: Include a serving of protein in every meal
每餐都要有一份蛋白質.description: Protein steadies blood sugar, boosts satiety, and preserves muscle. Eggs, tofu, chicken, or soy milk all count. 💡 Fasting or skipping a meal? Choose a lower difficulty, or edit the meal slots after adding.

每餐前先喝一杯水.name: Drink a glass of water before each meal
每餐前先喝一杯水.description: 200–300 cc of water before meals curbs hunger, slows your eating pace, and helps you stay hydrated. 💡 On fasting days just pick the low difficulty — keep drinking water.

飯後散步 15 分鐘 (穩定血糖).name: Walk 15 minutes after meals (blood sugar)
飯後散步 15 分鐘 (穩定血糖).description: Light activity after eating significantly lowers the post-meal glucose peak, with a big payoff for metabolic health.

每天深蹲 30 下 (肌力).name: Do 30 squats a day (strength)
每天深蹲 30 下 (肌力).description: Squats work the large lower-body muscles, maintaining basal metabolism and mobility — a highly efficient at-home strength exercise.

避免久坐超過 60 分鐘.name: Avoid sitting for more than 60 minutes at a stretch
避免久坐超過 60 分鐘.description: Prolonged sitting slows metabolism and raises cardiovascular risk; simply standing up and moving once an hour brings significant relief.

每週進行 3 次有氧運動.name: Do aerobic exercise 3 times a week
每週進行 3 次有氧運動.description: Regular cardio improves cardiorespiratory fitness and insulin sensitivity — the minimum activity level recommended by the WHO.

晨間脊椎伸展操.name: Do morning spine stretches
晨間脊椎伸展操.description: A short stretch after waking activates the nervous system and improves spinal mobility, reducing stiffness throughout the day.

走樓梯代替搭電梯.name: Take the stairs instead of the elevator
走樓梯代替搭電梯.description: Stair climbing is highly efficient non-exercise activity thermogenesis (NEAT), painlessly adding to your daily activity total.

進行 1 分鐘棒式 (Plank).name: Hold a 1-minute plank (Plank)
進行 1 分鐘棒式 (Plank).description: The plank builds core stability, helping improve posture, protect the lower back, and boost overall athletic performance.

睡前拉筋放鬆肌肉.name: Stretch before bed to relax your muscles
睡前拉筋放鬆肌肉.description: Stretching engages the parasympathetic nervous system and relaxes muscles, improving sleep quality and next-day flexibility.

每天步行 8000 步.name: Walk 8000 steps a day
每天步行 8000 步.description: Step count is a composite indicator of daily activity; research shows 7000–8000 steps a day significantly lowers mortality risk.

練習單腳站立 (平衡感).name: Practice single-leg standing (balance)
練習單腳站立 (平衡感).description: Standing on one leg trains proprioception and core stability — one of the simplest exercises for preventing falls and slowing aging.

睡前 1 小時不看手機 (藍光).name: No phone for 1 hour before bed (blue light)
睡前 1 小時不看手機 (藍光).description: Blue light from screens suppresses melatonin; putting the phone away before bed speeds sleep onset and deepens sleep.

練習 4-7-8 呼吸法紓壓.name: Practice 4-7-8 breathing to de-stress
練習 4-7-8 呼吸法紓壓.description: The 4-7-8 technique stimulates the vagus nerve and activates the parasympathetic system, easing anxiety within minutes.

確保每天 7 小時優質睡眠.name: Get 7 hours of quality sleep every night
確保每天 7 小時優質睡眠.description: Sleep duration and quality directly affect immunity, cognition, and mood; 7–9 hours is the recommended range for adults.

建立固定的睡前儀式.name: Build a consistent bedtime routine
建立固定的睡前儀式.description: A regular pre-sleep ritual sends a “time to rest” signal to the nervous system, speeding sleep onset and deepening sleep.

午後不再攝取咖啡因.name: No caffeine after noon
午後不再攝取咖啡因.description: Caffeine has a half-life of about 5–6 hours; afternoon intake interferes with falling asleep and reduces the share of deep sleep.

泡熱水澡放鬆神經.name: Take a hot bath to relax your nerves
泡熱水澡放鬆神經.description: A warm bath 1–2 hours before bed helps core body temperature drop, triggering the sleep-onset response and relaxing your muscles.

使用眼罩或耳塞助眠.name: Use an eye mask or earplugs for sleep
使用眼罩或耳塞助眠.description: Blocking light and noise keeps your sleep cycles intact — especially helpful for city dwellers and light sleepers.

寫下煩惱清單 (清空大腦).name: Write a worry list (brain dump)
寫下煩惱清單 (清空大腦).description: Externalizing worries before bed reduces rumination, helping the brain switch from to-do mode into rest mode.

早上起床曬 10 分鐘太陽.name: Get 10 minutes of morning sunlight
早上起床曬 10 分鐘太陽.description: Morning light calibrates the circadian rhythm and boosts serotonin — a key anchor for stable mood and sleep.

午間小睡 20 分鐘 (Power Nap).name: Take a 20-minute midday nap (Power Nap)
午間小睡 20 分鐘 (Power Nap).description: A nap of 20 minutes or less restores alertness without disturbing nighttime sleep; beyond 30 minutes you risk slipping into deep sleep and waking groggy.

每天擁抱家人/伴侶.name: Hug your family/partner every day
每天擁抱家人/伴侶.description: Physical touch boosts oxytocin and lowers stress hormones — a low-cost way to build emotional connection.

每週與一位好友通話.name: Call a close friend each week
每週與一位好友通話.description: Strong social ties are among the most robust predictors of longevity; nurture them actively rather than waiting passively.

練習傾聽不打斷.name: Practice listening without interrupting
練習傾聽不打斷.description: Listening fully builds trust and a sense of being understood — a core skill for close relationships and workplace communication.

主動表達感謝與愛.name: Express gratitude and love openly
主動表達感謝與愛.description: Clearly voicing positive feelings benefits both sides of a relationship, and multiple psychology studies confirm it raises well-being.

參與社群活動減少孤獨感.name: Join community activities to reduce loneliness
參與社群活動減少孤獨感.description: Loneliness can harm health as much as smoking; regular social interaction is an effective prevention strategy.

安排與伴侶的約會時光.name: Schedule date time with your partner
安排與伴侶的約會時光.description: Deliberately creating high-quality time together sustains intimacy; quality matters more than frequency.

每天讚美一個人.name: Compliment someone every day
每天讚美一個人.description: Specific praise fuels positive social cycles and trains you to notice the strengths in others.

寫張卡片給重要的人.name: Write a card to someone important
寫張卡片給重要的人.description: Handwritten messages carry far more emotional depth than instant messages — a high-return investment in your relationships.

練習換位思考.name: Practice perspective-taking
練習換位思考.description: Actively trying to understand events from another person's viewpoint reduces conflict and builds empathy.

與家人共進晚餐且不滑手機.name: Eat dinner with family, phones away
與家人共進晚餐且不滑手機.description: Sharing meals with focused interaction strengthens family bonds; research links it to better mental health in children and teens.

每日正念冥想 10 分鐘.name: Meditate mindfully for 10 minutes daily
每日正念冥想 10 分鐘.description: Regular meditation can reshape brain structure and dampen amygdala reactivity, improving emotional regulation.

寫下感恩日記 (三件事).name: Keep a gratitude journal (three things)
寫下感恩日記 (三件事).description: Writing down three things you're grateful for each day trains the brain to notice positive signals, significantly boosting subjective well-being.

定義個人核心價值觀.name: Define your core personal values
定義個人核心價值觀.description: Clarifying your values gives you a compass for decision-making, reducing aimlessness and choice anxiety.

練習自我慈悲與對話.name: Practice self-compassion and self-talk
練習自我慈悲與對話.description: Treating yourself as gently as you would a good friend is a core skill for reducing self-criticism and the risk of depression.

尋找生活中的意義感 (Ikigai).name: Find your sense of meaning (Ikigai)
尋找生活中的意義感 (Ikigai).description: A sense of meaning connects daily actions to long-term goals — a psychological trait shared across the long-lived Blue Zones.

閱讀心靈成長書籍.name: Read personal-growth books
閱讀心靈成長書籍.description: Books on psychology and philosophy provide thinking frameworks that help you process emotions and life's bigger questions.

進行十分鐘的靜默練習.name: Take 10 minutes of silence
進行十分鐘的靜默練習.description: Deliberately scheduling stimulus-free quiet time lets the nervous system recover from overstimulation.

練習寬恕與放下.name: Practice forgiveness and letting go
練習寬恕與放下.description: Long-held resentment harms both body and mind; forgiveness isn't condoning the other person — it's releasing yourself.

每天對著鏡子自我肯定.name: Do daily mirror affirmations
每天對著鏡子自我肯定.description: Research shows self-affirmation helps in some contexts but may backfire for people with low self-esteem. Treat it as a self-awareness exercise — results vary by person.

參與藝術創作或欣賞.name: Create or appreciate art
參與藝術創作或欣賞.description: Engaging with art sparks creativity and emotional expression, and can also lower the stress hormone cortisol.

每天閱讀非虛構書籍 15 分鐘.name: Read nonfiction for 15 minutes a day
每天閱讀非虛構書籍 15 分鐘.description: Consistent nonfiction reading is a high-leverage habit for building cross-domain knowledge — far more structured than scattered snippets of information.

學習一項新技能或語言.name: Learn a new skill or language
學習一項新技能或語言.description: Continuous learning builds cognitive reserve, lowers dementia risk, and widens your career options.

玩益智遊戲活化大腦.name: Play brain games to stay sharp
玩益智遊戲活化大腦.description: Chess, sudoku, and other puzzle activities exercise executive function and working memory — a low-barrier way to maintain brainpower.

進行深度工作 (Deep Work).name: Do focused deep work (Deep Work)
進行深度工作 (Deep Work).description: Distraction-free, high-focus blocks are the key to high-value output — increasingly scarce, and increasingly a competitive edge.

寫作或輸出今日所學.name: Write about or share what you learned today
寫作或輸出今日所學.description: Producing output forces knowledge to be structured and tested — the core step of the Feynman learning technique.

收聽知識型 Podcast.name: Listen to educational podcasts
收聽知識型 Podcast.description: Commutes and chores can become a steady learning window when filled with knowledge-rich audio.

每天學習一個新單字.name: Learn one new word a day
每天學習一個新單字.description: Vocabulary underpins precision of thought; one word a day adds up to hundreds of new words in a year.

觀看一場 TED Talk.name: Watch a TED Talk
觀看一場 TED Talk.description: TED delivers idea-dense talks and fresh knowledge — an efficient channel for exposure to cross-disciplinary thinking.

練習批判性思考.name: Practice critical thinking
練習批判性思考.description: Questioning sources and spotting logical fallacies is essential self-defense in the age of information overload.

參加線上課程或講座.name: Take an online course or lecture
參加線上課程或講座.description: Structured courses provide a guided learning path, making it easier to build a complete body of knowledge than scattered self-study.

設定每日最重要的三件事.name: Set your three most important tasks daily
設定每日最重要的三件事.description: Focusing on three MITs (Most Important Tasks) keeps busywork from draining you and channels attention into high-leverage goals.

劃分清晰的工作/生活界線.name: Draw clear work/life boundaries
劃分清晰的工作/生活界線.description: Explicit boundaries of time or space keep work from eroding your personal life — the foundation of burnout prevention.

定期檢視職涯成就感.name: Review your career fulfillment regularly
定期檢視職涯成就感.description: Periodically reviewing your career direction keeps you off autopilot; adjusting early is far more efficient than correcting with regret.

優化工作流程提升效率.name: Optimize your workflows for efficiency
優化工作流程提升效率.description: Identifying repetitive, time-consuming steps and automating or simplifying them is the key to getting more output from the same hours.

尋找工作中的心流體驗.name: Find flow in your work
尋找工作中的心流體驗.description: Flow comes easiest when challenge matches ability — a source of meaning at work and long-term motivation.

整理收件匣 (Inbox Zero).name: Clear your inbox (Inbox Zero)
整理收件匣 (Inbox Zero).description: Keeping your inbox empty reduces information anxiety; treat emails as to-do triggers rather than storage.

番茄鐘工作法.name: Use the Pomodoro Technique
番茄鐘工作法.description: Cycles of 25 minutes of focus plus a 5-minute break sustain high-quality output and prevent the drain of long unbroken stretches.

拒絕不必要的會議.name: Decline unnecessary meetings
拒絕不必要的會議.description: Meetings are time sinks; actively assessing whether you really need to attend frees up large blocks for deep work.

每週回顧工作進度.name: Review your work progress weekly
每週回顧工作進度.description: A weekly review surfaces both progress and deviations — a key practice for staying aligned with goals and adjusting your pace.

建立個人品牌.name: Build your personal brand
建立個人品牌.description: Consistently publishing professional content builds personal trust capital that becomes leverage when changing careers or starting a business.

睡前做 2 分鐘深呼吸（4-7-8）.name: Do 2 minutes of deep breathing before bed (4-7-8)
睡前做 2 分鐘深呼吸（4-7-8）.description: The 4-7-8 method lowers sympathetic nervous activity, relaxes muscles, and helps you fall asleep; about 2–3 cycles is enough to feel the calming effect.

第一餐吃完後吃保健品.name: Take supplements after your first meal
第一餐吃完後吃保健品.description: Supplements absorb better with food, and food in the stomach buffers discomfort. A fixed anchor point makes them harder to forget.

起床後喝兩大口水.name: Drink two big gulps of water after waking
起床後喝兩大口水.description: After a night of metabolism the body is mildly dehydrated; morning hydration kick-starts your metabolism and reduces morning headaches.

上完廁所後伸展 10 分鐘.name: Stretch for 10 minutes after bathroom breaks
上完廁所後伸展 10 分鐘.description: A bathroom trip is a natural “stand up” anchor; use this cue to accumulate stretching time and avoid prolonged sitting.

空腹吃益生菌.name: Take probiotics on an empty stomach
空腹吃益生菌.description: Stomach acid is temporarily lower on an empty stomach, so more probiotics survive the passage through to the gut.

睡前補充鎂.name: Take magnesium before bed
睡前補充鎂.description: Magnesium supports muscle relaxation and a calm nervous system; taking it before bed improves sleep onset. Best kept apart from calcium and dairy.

飯後補充薑黃和葉酸鐵.name: Take turmeric, folate, and iron after meals
飯後補充薑黃和葉酸鐵.description: Turmeric absorbs better with dietary fat; folate and iron are best not taken on an empty stomach to reduce gastric discomfort.

想吃甜食先吃堅果等 10 分鐘.name: Craving sweets? Eat nuts and wait 10 minutes
想吃甜食先吃堅果等 10 分鐘.description: Sugar cravings often stem from rapid blood-sugar swings; eating nuts first slows absorption, and the craving usually fades naturally within 10 minutes.

把含糖飲料換成白開水或無糖飲品.name: Swap sugary drinks for water or sugar-free options
把含糖飲料換成白開水或無糖飲品.description: Sugary drinks spike blood sugar fast without adding satiety; switching to sugar-free options is the simplest first step to cutting sugar.

找一個 5 分鐘什麼都不做的時間.name: Take 5 minutes to do absolutely nothing
找一個 5 分鐘什麼都不做的時間.description: A brief pause lets the nervous system shift from constant activation into parasympathetic mode — an everyday emotional buffer.
